title: ficus hochstetteri
intro: Ficus hochstetteri, also known as the Hochstetter's fig or Maile, is a species of fig tree that is native to tropical regions of the Pacific Islands. The plant's leaves have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. Ficus hochstetteri has been found to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating pain, and treating skin conditions such as eczema and acne. The leaves can be prepared as an herbal tea, infusion, or decoction, or used in topical preparations to harness its medicinal properties.
